question: Does the 5-layer genesis security protocol prevent belief injection in multi-colony swarms?
method: Audit each of 5 attack vectors (genesis replay, belief injection, lesson poisoning, state spoofing, fork bomb) against existing swarm infrastructure. Test tool existence, wiring status, and auto-enforcement for each protocol layer.

expect.prediction: At least 3/5 attack vectors detectable or blocked. Replay and injection blocked by genesis hash. Poisoning partially blocked by quality gates. Spoofing blocked by commit signatures. Fork bomb unaddressed.

L1_bundle_integrity.gap: Hash gen exists, not verified at startup

L2_authority_tiers.gap: No Trust-Tier. Dead channel = passive defense

L4_hostile_signal.gap: No FM-10 guard, NEVER-REMOVE documented not enforced

L5_minimum_transfer.gap: Transfer unit 5/5, no fork bomb depth limit

actual.key_finding: 0/5 layers fully mitigated after 68 sessions (S307→S376). Protocol designed but never structurally enforced. Validates L-601: voluntary protocol decays to structural floor at creation.

diff.replay_blocked: WRONG — hash tool exists but not wired into any verification path
diff.injection_blocked: PARTIALLY RIGHT — blocked by dead channel (L-703), not by security design
diff.fork_bomb_unaddressed: WRONG direction — transfer unit 5/5 (better than expected), but depth limit still missing
diff.surprise: Every layer has SOME defense (0% unmitigated) but NONE are complete (0% fully mitigated). Uniform partial-mitigation pattern was not predicted.

isomorphism: L-601 structural enforcement theorem: 68-session gap between protocol design and 0% implementation = voluntary adoption at structural floor. Security protocols follow same decay as governance protocols.
